level_signals: Owns multiple high-leverage product areas across mobile, platform, and growth
Expected to engage directly with enterprise customers and drive roadmap independently

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: 5+ years of product management experience, with meaningful time in at least one of: mobile/native applications, front-end platform, or growth and lifecycle PM.
Track record of shipping mobile or platform-layer products with real impact on adoption, retention, or engagement.
Strong working knowledge of mobile UI/UX principles, such as iOS HIG and Material Design, and comfort making tradeoffs between native and cross-platform approaches.
Analytical by default: you define metrics before you build, know how to instrument a product, and can run and interpret A/B tests.
Strong cross-functional collaborator who works well with distributed engineering and design teams and can translate technical tradeoffs for non-technical stakeholders.
Highly execution-oriented with a bias for getting things shipped and iterating from there.

nice_to_haves.raw_text_from_jd: Experience owning front-end platform work: design systems, white-labeling, shared component libraries, or configuration infrastructure for enterprise SaaS.
Experience with enterprise mobile compliance requirements: MDM/MAM, enterprise app distribution, data encryption at rest and in transit.
technologies_named: data encryption at rest and in transit

nice_to_haves.raw_text_from_jd: Exposure to agentic AI systems, LLMs, or conversational interfaces, ideally in a mobile or desktop context.

scope.surface: North platform — iOS, Android, macOS apps, front-end shared component layer, and user onboarding/retention/growth lifecycle
primary_metrics_inferred: Activation rate across new enterprise deployments
core_responsibilities: Define and execute the roadmap for North's iOS, Android, and macOS apps, balancing platform-native capabilities with cross-platform consistency.
Own the shared component and configuration layer that enables enterprise customization
Design and iterate on FTUX and onboarding flows that minimize time-to-value and drive activation across new enterprise deployments.
Own analytics instrumentation to track funnel performance, feature adoption, and re-engagement; run structured experiments to improve key metrics.
Engage directly with enterprise customers to surface use cases across surfaces and validate product decisions with qualitative and quantitative evidence.
Collaborate with design and engineering to maintain a coherent, accessible component system and ensure pixel-perfect experiences that scale across customer deployments.

green_flags: Clear product ownership across three distinct but related domains (native apps, platform, growth)
Remote-eligible with hybrid options in multiple major cities (SF, NYC, Toronto)
Generous benefits: 6 weeks vacation, 100% parental leave top-up, full health and dental
Explicit encouragement to apply even if not every qualification is met
Equity options mentioned as part of compensation
Clear and specific responsibilities with measurable outcomes implied
red_flags: Role spans three distinct product domains (mobile, platform, growth) which may indicate an under-resourced PM function or unclear ownership boundaries
Preference for EST working hours noted but not clearly required — ambiguous expectation for candidates in other time zones
Office time expectations are vague: 'hybrid' mentioned but no specific in-office frequency stated

fields_inferred_not_stated: seniority.level (inferred from 5+ years requirement and scope of ownership)
scope.business_model (inferred from product description of North as enterprise agentic AI platform)
scope.primary_metrics_inferred (inferred from responsibilities; not explicitly stated in JD)
compensation.equity_form (inferred from 'equity options' mentioned under Why Join section)
location.remote_us_eligible (inferred from 'Remote or hybrid' language in location section)
ambiguities: Location says 'Remote or hybrid (San Francisco, New York, Toronto)' and separately mentions preference for EST hours — unclear whether remote means fully remote globally or US/Canada remote only
The perks section mentions offices in Toronto, New York, San Francisco, London, and Paris — but the role location only lists SF, NY, Toronto; London and Paris not listed as role-eligible locations
The role blends Growth PM and Technical PM responsibilities across three domains; archetype is set to Growth PM but Technical PM is a strong secondary fit
